tweets.text:
Minh Duy Tong Nguyen aka Tony Playboy, of Vietnam, was once a fugitive wanted for the murder of two people and the attempted murder of three others. He was featured on an episode of "America’s Most Wanted" in 1990 and subsequently captured in Canada and extradited to the U.S. to stand trial for murder. 

He was sentenced to 45-years in the state prison.

ICE @EROHouston took custody of him April 16, upon his release from prison.

His deportation order is over 25 years old.

He’s about to go home.

ICE arrested him upon his release from prison and removed him to his home nation May 4. https://t.co/CiXQquRACL

tweets.text:
👟In 2018, Antonio Efrain Morales-Diego, of Guatemala, told Border Patrol agents that he snuck into the U.S. on foot near San Luis, Ariz. 

➡️Border Patrol released him on an order of recognizance and issued him a notice to appear.

Morales-Diego made his way to Virginia where he was later convicted of abduction and strangulation.

ICE arrested him upon his release from a Virginia correctional facility based on:
⚖️INA § 237(a)(2)(A)(iii): Alien convicted of an aggravated felony
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Miguel Solis-Martinez, of Honduras, was arrested in 2008 for soliciting someone to murder his spouse in Bexar County, Texas. He was sentenced to a decade behind bars.

🔎Solis snuck into the country in 1999 near San Ysidro, Calif., and was put into removal proceedings. An immigration judge granted him voluntary departure in 2002 — in other words, he agreed to leave the country on his own rather than get deported. He didn’t leave though.

In addition to the homicide-related conviction, his rap sheet also includes:
➡️Illegal re-entry conviction
➡️Forgery charge
➡️DUI arrest
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Police say DNA evidence links Luis Benitez-Gonzalez of Mexico to the murders of two women in Texas.

🔎These murdered took place six years apart, and investigators believe there may be more victims. 

ICE lodged a detainer with the Dallas County Jail to ensure he is not released back into the public while he faces justice for the alleged murders. 

➡️Dallas officials honor ICE detainers, which keeps their community safer from criminal illegal aliens.

tweets.text:
Ricardo Crespo-Ruiz, a criminal illegal alien from Mexico, was convicted in 2002 for VOLUNTARY MANSLAUGHTER and BURGLARY in Los Angeles and deported following his 11-year sentence.

In 2025, he tried driving back into the U.S. with a fake passport he says he purchased for $500. That fail with our @CBP partners cost him another 15 months in prison.

ICE @EROLosAngeles ensured he had a ride upon his release May 8 that will eventually land back on his side of the border.

tweets.text:
Brenda Liliana Rivera-Estrada was arrested by police in Arizona on charges of manslaughter and child abuse, but Maricopa county prosecutors declined to file charges.

ICE officers picked her up pursuant to an honored detainer, and she will be held in ICE detention pending a review of her case by the DOJ’s Executive Office for Immigration Review.

Read more⬇️

tweets.text:
Illegal alien Wenceslao O. Panameno was deported back to El Salvador by ICE in 2017 following a DUI and five-year sentence for RAPING A CHILD in Massachusetts.

Then, a 2025 ICE tipline call led our Boston officers and HSI special agents to a residence where they reintroduced themselves to Panameno, who says he snuck in using a passport with someone else’s name. 

He’s now back in our custody following a year in prison for illegal reentry and will be deported soonest.

📞Your tips make America safer: (866) 347-2423.

tweets.text:
Henry Roldan Perez-Roblero, 21, of Guatemala, faces a felony charge in Florida for allegedly sexually assaulting a child under 12 — a capital felony punishable in the state by death.

ICE issued a detainer with law enforcement in Martin County, Florida, and will take custody of him in the event he is ever released. 

👉Unlike sanctuary jurisdiction officials, Florida officials honor our detainers.

Perez-Roblero illegally entered the United States in Arizona in January 2021 and was released into the country. An immigration judge ordered him removed January 22, 2025.
